mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the equity discount rate and signals easier financial conditions, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months following a FEDFUNDS decline over a 2-year rolling window, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and lower earnings expectations, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not lower than the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise together, stronger retail spending and payroll growth indicate robust final demand, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS up and PAYEMS up fails to outperform either series alone in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of joint-growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial activity and improved cyclical earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive INDPRO increases do not correspond to above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 50% over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration while FEDFUNDS remains low indicates inflation pressure without offsetting policy restraint, which should raise real-rate and margin concerns and push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is below 2%
failure_condition: Reject if the low-rate inflation-acceleration condition does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST signals firmer housing demand and construction activity, which improves growth expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-strength triggers do not lead to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes negative in a majority of triggered observations across a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth combined with falling UNRATE indicates genuine labor-market tightening and stronger consumer income, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-expansion and unemployment-decline condition does not outperform either input alone, or if positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-favorable months.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 contracts while RSAFS is weak, the economy is likely entering a demand recession that compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines in the latest quarter and RSAFS is below its 3-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if contraction episodes do not yield below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate for negative returns is under 55% over any 5-year rolling window.
